risks: Frame mapping ambiguity: MetroCity has 24 columns per direction but we need exactly 7 frames (3 walk + 2 type + 2 read). The builder will need to visually inspect the spritesheet and make judgment calls on which frames map to which animations.
MetroCity frame size is 32x32 vs current 16x32. This is a 2x width increase. Character positioning (anchor at bottom-center, sitting offsets) may need tuning.
Compositing complexity at load time: layering body + hair + outfit via canvas for 13 agents x 7 frames x 3 directions = 273 composite operations. Needs a pre-composition strategy.
over_engineering_flags: Auto-assignment for new users with exactly 13 fixed agents is over-engineering — a static mapping table is sufficient.
Runtime compositing is more complex than build-time pre-compositing via a Node script.
challenges.recommendation: Simplify. Use a build-time Node script to pre-composite 13 agent spritesheets into static PNGs matching the current format. Runtime stays unchanged.

projects.title: MetroCity Composable Character System
projects.scope_summary: Build a Node/canvas script that composites MetroCity base body + hair + outfit layers into per-agent spritesheets. Map MetroCity's 24-column layout to the existing 7-frame x 3-direction format. Pre-compose 13 unique agent combinations with distinct skin/hair/outfit. Output char_0..12.png matching current format so asset-loader and renderer work unchanged. Copy MetroCity source assets to public/assets/metrocity/ for the build script.
